program_name: Digital Humanities - Interactive Systems and Digital Media
teaching_language: English and Italian (the 'Affective Computing, Arts and Cultural Welfare' (ACW) curriculum is taught in English; the 'Interaction and Digital Creative Production' (IPC) curriculum is taught in Italian)
campus: Genoa (ACW curriculum, English) and Savona (IPC curriculum, Italian)
admission_type: free access with verification of curricular requirements and assessment of personal readiness (document-based, with grade-based exemption)

academic_requirements.summary: Free-access master's degree (120 CFU, 2 years) requiring a Bachelor degree plus verification of curricular CFU requirements and an assessment of personal readiness. The English-taught in-scope curriculum is ACW (Affective Computing, Arts and Cultural Welfare) in Genoa.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: A Bachelor's degree or an equivalent qualification recognised as suitable.
academic_requirements.general_curricular_credits: A minimum of 6 CFU in one or more of the following SSD areas: Computer Science (INF/01, ING-INF/05); Mathematics (MAT/02-06); Architecture/Design (ICAR/13, ICAR/17); Arts/Literature (L-ART/03-07; L-FIL-LET/10-12; L-LIN/11-12); Philosophy/Psychology (M-FIL/05; M-PSI/01); Social Sciences (SPS/07-08, SECS-P/07-08, SECS-S/01).
academic_requirements.curriculum_specific_credits: ACW (English curriculum): at least 18 CFU in computing, OR at least 18 CFU in arts/cognitive fields plus a minimum of 6 CFU in computing. IPC (Italian curriculum): at least 18 total CFU in INF/01, ING-INF/05 (Path 1) or fewer credits with supplementary coursework (Path 2).
academic_requirements.grade_threshold_for_exemption: Automatic confirmation of adequate preparation for candidates with a bachelor (Italian or foreign) final grade of at least 9/10 of the maximum, or a final grade corresponding to ECTS classification 'A'.
academic_requirements.international_qualification: Non-EU candidates residing abroad apply through UniGeApply for pre-evaluation of curricular requirements before enrolment.

language_requirements.required_level: ACW (English curriculum): English knowledge at a level no lower than B2. IPC (Italian curriculum): English at a level no lower than B1, plus Italian B2 for international students.
accepted_proofs: [uncertain] The programme page states the required English level (B2 for ACW) but directs to the Polytechnic School / recognised-certificates list for accepted certificate types; specific tests (IELTS/TOEFL) and scores are not enumerated on the page.
For international applicants choosing the Italian IPC curriculum, a B2-level Italian proficiency certificate is required.
exemption_rules: [uncertain] The page does not describe a specific English-certificate exemption beyond the recognised-certificates route; reconfirm accepted proofs on the dedicated list.
language_requirements.assessment_or_upload_stage: English (and, for IPC, Italian) proficiency is verified during the assessment of personal readiness at pre-registration; international applicants verify it during UniGeApply pre-evaluation.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: Assessment of adequacy of personal readiness (no TOLC; a document-based verification by the committee). NOT a TOLC.
entry_exam_or_test.mandatory: Mandatory only for candidates who do not meet the grade-based automatic confirmation.
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: Applicants to the Digital Humanities master who do not qualify for the automatic grade exemption.
entry_exam_or_test.format: The committee verifies preparation based on documentation provided at pre-registration, evaluating academic potential (GPA, class rank), relevance and quality of the bachelor's degree and awarding university, motivation letter, CV and professional experience, and optional reference letters (and Italian-language adequacy for the IPC track).
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: [uncertain] Automatic confirmation applies at a bachelor grade of at least 9/10 of the maximum (or ECTS 'A'); otherwise the candidate must exceed the scoring threshold defined in the Educational Regulations, which is not stated numerically on the page.
entry_exam_or_test.interview: No interview is described; the assessment is document-based.

rounds: Spring session for EU citizens and non-EU residents in Italy (UniGeApply): opens 9 April 2026; English-taught master's degrees close 28 August 2026.
Open-access master pre-registration on the UniGe calendar: from 9 April to 30 November 2026, and until 10 March 2027 with late payment.
application_deadline_eu.enrolment_deadline: Confirmation of enrolment (first year): from 15 July 2026 to 30 November 2026, and until 10 March 2027 with late payment. Enrolment in years after the first: from 15 July to 5 October 2026, with arrears from 6 October to 5 November 2026.
application_deadline_eu.notes: The programme deadlines page lists uniform open-access enrolment dates and does not separate EU vs non-EU first-year windows.

rounds: Fall session for non-EU candidates requiring a visa (UniGeApply): opens 26 November 2025; institution-wide close 20 March 2026; pre-evaluation results by 22 May 2026.
application_deadline_non_eu.visa_or_pre_enrolment_deadline: After UniGeApply eligibility, visa applicants complete the Universitaly pre-enrolment and apply for a study visa.
application_deadline_non_eu.early_closure: [uncertain] No programme-specific early closure is published for the Digital Humanities master; the only listed fall early closures are Medical-Pharmaceutical Biotechnology (master, 13 Feb 2026), Maritime Science and Technology (bachelor, 18 Feb 2026) and Computer Engineering (bachelor, 8 Mar 2026).
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: Non-EU visa applicants must use the fall session via UniGeApply; the A.Y. 2026/27 fall window has now closed (closed 20 March 2026). The English ACW curriculum is the in-scope English-taught option for non-EU applicants.

required_documents.notes: Uploaded on UniGeApply (max 2 MB per document); also evaluated in the personal-readiness assessment.
If still in progress, the degree must be completed by 15 August 2026.

required_documents.document_name: Transcript of records (exams with credits and grades)
required_documents.notes: Used to verify the curricular CFU/SSD requirements (general 6 CFU plus curriculum-specific credits).
Quality of the motivation letter is one of the readiness-assessment criteria.
Optional; considered by the committee in the readiness assessment.

required_documents.document_name: English language proficiency certificate (B2 for ACW) and, for IPC international students, Italian B2 certificate
required_documents.notes: ACW requires English B2; IPC requires English B1 plus Italian B2 for international students. Accepted certificate types per the recognised-certificates list.

required_documents.document_name: Valid passport (non-EU) and visa/pre-enrolment documents via Universitaly
required_documents.notes: Non-EU visa applicants complete Universitaly pre-enrolment after UniGeApply eligibility; foreign qualifications require Declaration of Value or CIMEA Statement of Comparability.

source_quotes.quote: DIGITAL HUMANITIES - INTERACTIVE SYSTEMS AND DIGITAL MEDIA, Master's Degree, Class LM-92 R (Communication), 120 CFU, 2 years; tracks in Genova (Affective Computing, Arts and Cultural Welfare) and Savona.
ACW curriculum: at least 18 CFU in computing OR at least 18 CFU in arts/cognitive fields plus a minimum 6 CFU in computing; general requirement minimum 6 CFU in the listed SSD areas.
ACW curriculum: knowledge of the English language at a level no lower than B2; IPC curriculum: knowledge of the English language at a level no lower than B1.
Automatic pass with a bachelor's degree, Italian or foreign, with a grade of at least 9/10 of the maximum prescribed, or a final grade corresponding to ECTS 'A'.
Spring session opens 9 April 2026; Master's degrees close 28 August 2026. Fall session (non-EU visa applicants) opens 26 November 2025, closes 20 March 2026.

uncertainty_notes: The programme is bilingual: the in-scope English-taught curriculum is ACW (Affective Computing, Arts and Cultural Welfare) in Genoa; the IPC curriculum is taught in Italian in Savona. Non-EU international applicants seeking the English route should select ACW.
Accepted English certificate types and scores (IELTS/TOEFL) are not enumerated on the programme page; they are defined on the University's recognised-certificates list.
No numerical readiness-assessment score threshold is published beyond the grade exemption; it is set in the Educational Regulations.
No programme-specific fall early-closure date is published for this master.
The programme deadlines page gives the generic open-access enrolment calendar and does not separate EU vs non-EU first-year application windows; those come from the institution-wide UniGeApply pages.
